zavadovsky-v-republic-of-austria-et-al-2.lesson: Pro-se fake-citation warnings set the baseline courts now apply to represented parties.

feldman-trost-v-district-6-board-of-educ.lesson: Verify every quoted phrase against the opinion text — courts catch the wording, not just the reporter cite.

edward-c-allen-v-western-governors-unive.lesson: Dismissal with prejudice plus token costs shows courts will end cases built on hallucinated authority.

hampton-v-lofton-2026-03-31.lesson: Never manufacture record citations — appellate courts read the trial order and will call hallucinations by name.

leonard-colbert-v-county-of-riverside-20.lesson: Confirm every quoted statutory provision against the code — courts now name AI when statutes don't read as quoted.

rushing-v-rushing-et-al-2026-03-31.lesson: Appellate courts will affirm dismissal outright when AI-fabricated authority drives the briefing.

jaii-leshawn-quinones-v-unknown-love-et-.lesson: Show-cause orders are now the reflex response to fabricated reporter citations — respond with full AI disclosure.

christopher-a-ambrose-v-bandy-x-lee-2026.lesson: Courts explicitly warn that AI-hallucinated propositions invite Rule 11 sanctions even before monetary orders issue.

mohamed-hussain-et-al-v-mansoor-quraishi.lesson: Eight fabricated citations in one brief will trigger show-cause — build a verification log before filing.

heimkes-v-fairhope-motorcoach-resort-con.lesson: CoCounsel output is not presumptively accurate — a $55K sanction plus bar referral proves vendor pedigree is no shield.

elbert-coleman-iii-elbert-coleman-iv-v-n.lesson: Repealed statutes are the tell of AI drafting — always confirm statutory status before citing.

chelsea-montes-v-suns-legacy-partners-ll.lesson: Mandatory CLE plus costs attaches once fabricated citations appear across multiple filings — one audit would catch the pattern.

nguedi-v-general-dynamics-information-te.lesson: Fabricated quotes are caught on cross-check against the actual opinion — read the case, don't trust the snippet.

evgeny-yaroshevsky-v-karlin-et-al-2026-0.lesson: Consumer chatbots like Gemini are not research tools — ban them from the legal research workflow outright.

quandel-construction-group-inc-v-hunt-co.lesson: Match case name, docket number, and quoted language to the opinion itself — mismatches now generate show-cause orders.

federal-trade-commission-v-james-d-nolan.lesson: Even unnamed hallucinations draw judicial warnings — treat any AI-assisted filing as audit-required.

rosa-maria-cabrera-thompson-v-seattle-pu.lesson: Docket-number mismatches are an instant tell — courts pull the record and issue show-cause the same day.

syreeta-lashawn-mcneal-v-vera-l-calvin-e.lesson: Repeat citations to a nonexistent opinion escalate from warning to show-cause fast — pull every cite before filing.

souza-v-city-of-fitchburg-2026-03-30.lesson: Telltale AI phrasing addressing the filer in second person tips courts off — strip all AI artifacts from drafts.

williams-v-chicago-board-of-education-20.lesson: A single 'totally made-up quotation' in a response brief now triggers a show-cause — verify every quoted line against the opinion.
